tool.name: SameStr Compare
tool.description: performs pairwise clade-specific comparison of metagenomic alignments

param.name: input_snvs
param.label: Input SNV profiles
param.help: A (multisample) SNV profile typically generated with SameStr Filter.

param.name: input_names
param.label: Sample identifiers
param.help: Dataset listing the sample/reference genome identifiers one per line.

param.name: database
param.label: SameStr database
param.help: SameStr database built from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS markers.

param.name: minority_dominant
param.label: Type of variants to calculate/report metrics for
param.option: Use all variants (do not distinguish between minority and dominant variants)
Use all variants, but include separate metrics based on dominant variants

param.label: Output dominant variant alignments
param.help: Generate FASTA alignments of dominant variants for each clade (default: False).

tool.help:
@SAMESTR_INTRO@
SameStr Compare ...............
SameStr Compare compares samples within a single clade to identify shared bacterial strains. It measures this by calculating similarity metrics between their variant profiles to detect when the same bacterial strain is present across different samples.
**Input Requirements**
For each analysis, the following inputs must be provided:
- A multi-sample SNV profile in NPZ/NPY format generated by SameStr filter or SameStr merge. - A list of the samples present in the SNV profile in simple one-sample-per-line format as generated by SameStr filter or merge. - The MetaPhlAn or mOTUS clade marker database that matches the database used to generate the input profiles.
**Parameters**
- Type of variants to calculate/report metrics for: You can choose to either calculate and report metrics:
* based on all variants independent of whether they are minority or dominant ones * based exclusively on dominant variants * based on all variants (like in the first option), but include in the same matrices also the metrics calculated for dominant variants only.
This last option will add additional rows/columns to the different metrics outputs with headers of the form <sample>.dom.
- Output dominant variant alignments: Generate FASTA alignments of dominant variants for each clade.
**Results and Outputs**
The tool generates three to four outputs:
- Coverage overlap: A matrix of marker positions with overlapping coverage per sample pair. - Closest distance: Pairwise distance matrix for the samples based on their variant differences. - Similarity fraction: Pairwise fractional similarity matrix for the samples. - An (optional) alignment around dominant variants in FASTA format.
